segments.text: Lesson 45.

translation.text: 第45课。

segments.text: Of Men and Galaxies.

translation.text: 人类与星系。

segments.text: First listen and then answer the following question.

translation.text: 先听录音，然后回答下面的问题。

segments.text: What is the most influential factor in any human society?

translation.text: 任何人类社会中影响最大的因素是什么？

segments.text: In man's early days, competition with other creatures must have been critical.

translation.text: 在人类早期，与其他生物的竞争必定至关重要。

segments.text: But this phase of our development is now finished.

translation.text: 但我们发展的这一阶段如今已经结束。

segments.text: Indeed, we lack practice and experience nowadays in dealing with primitive conditions.

translation.text: 事实上，今天我们已经缺乏应对原始环境的实践和经验。

segments.text: I am sure that, without modern weapons, I would make a very poor show of disputing the ownership of a cave with a bear.

translation.text: 我确信，如果没有现代武器，我同熊争夺洞穴所有权时一定会表现得很糟。

segments.text: And in this I do not think that I stand alone.

translation.text: 我认为在这一点上并非只有我如此。

segments.text: The last creature to compete with man was the mosquito.

translation.text: 最后一种与人类竞争的生物是蚊子。

segments.text: But even the mosquito has been subdued by attention to drainage and by chemical sprays.

translation.text: 但就连蚊子也已通过改善排水和喷洒化学药剂得到控制。

segments.text: between ourselves, person against person, community against community, still persists, however, and it is as fierce as it ever was.

translation.text: 然而，人类内部个人与个人、群体与群体之间的竞争仍然存在，而且一如既往地激烈。

segments.text: But the competition of man against man is not the simple process envisioned in biology.

translation.text: 但人与人的竞争并非生物学所设想的简单过程。

segments.text: It is not a simple competition for a fixed amount of food determined by the physical environment, because the environment that determines our evolution is no longer essentially physical.

translation.text: 它不是争夺由物理环境决定的固定数量食物，因为决定我们进化的环境已不再主要是物理环境。

segments.text: Our environment is chiefly conditioned by the things we believe.

translation.text: 我们的环境主要由我们相信的事物所塑造。

segments.text: Morocco and California are bits of the Earth in very similar latitudes, both on the west coasts of continents with similar climates, and probably with rather similar natural resources.

translation.text: 摩洛哥和加利福尼亚是地球上纬度十分相近的地区，都位于大陆西岸，气候相似，自然资源大概也颇为相似。

segments.text: Yet their present development is wholly different.

translation.text: 然而，两地目前的发展完全不同。

segments.text: Not so much because of different people even, but because of the different thoughts that exist in the minds of their inhabitants.

translation.text: 这甚至不太是因为人民不同，而是因为居民头脑中的思想不同。

segments.text: This is the point I wish to emphasize.

translation.text: 这正是我要强调的一点。

segments.text: The most important factor in our environment is the state of our own minds.

translation.text: 我们环境中最重要的因素，是我们自身的思想状态。

segments.text: It is well known that where the white man has invaded a primitive culture, the most destructive effects have come not from physical weapons, but from ideas.

translation.text: 众所周知，白人入侵原始文化时，最具破坏性的影响并非来自物质武器，而是来自思想。

segments.text: Ideas are dangerous.

translation.text: 思想是危险的。

segments.text: The Holy Office knew this full well when it caused heretics to be burned in days gone by.

translation.text: 昔日宗教裁判所下令烧死异端时，对此再清楚不过。

segments.text: Indeed, the concept of free speech only exists in our modern society because when you are inside a community, you are conditioned by the conventions of the community to such a degree that it is very difficult to

translation.text: 事实上，言论自由的概念之所以只存在于现代社会，是因为人在一个共同体内部时，会受到共同体习俗如此深刻的制约，以至于很难

segments.text: conceive of anything really destructive.

translation.text: 构想出真正具有破坏性的东西。

segments.text: It is only someone looking on from outside that can inject the dangerous thoughts.

translation.text: 只有从外部观察的人才能注入危险思想。

segments.text: I do not doubt that it would be possible to inject ideas into the modern world that would utterly destroy us.

translation.text: 我毫不怀疑，向现代世界注入足以彻底毁灭我们的思想是可能的。

segments.text: I would like to give you an example, but fortunately, I cannot do so.

translation.text: 我想举个例子，幸运的是我做不到。

segments.text: Perhaps it will suffice to mention the nuclear bomb.

translation.text: 也许提到核弹就足够了。

segments.text: Imagine the effect on a reasonably advanced technological society, one that still does not possess the bomb, of making it aware of the possibility of supplying sufficient details to enable the thing to be constructed.

translation.text: 设想一个技术相当先进但尚未拥有核弹的社会：一旦让它知道核弹可以制造，并提供足以把核弹造出来的详细资料，会产生怎样的影响。

segments.text: Twenty or thirty pages of information handed to any of the major world powers around the year 1925 would have been sufficient to change the course of world history.

translation.text: 在1925年前后，只要把二三十页资料交给任何一个世界强国，就足以改变世界历史的进程。

segments.text: It is a strange thought, but I believe a correct one, that twenty or thirty pages of ideas and information would be capable of turning the present-day world upside down or even

translation.text: 二三十页思想和信息竟能把当今世界彻底颠覆，甚至

segments.text: destroying it.

translation.text: 毁灭它；这是个奇怪却正确的想法。

segments.text: I have often tried to conceive of what those pages might contain, but of course, I cannot do so because I am a prisoner of the present-day world, just as all of you are.

translation.text: 我常试图想象那些书页会写些什么，却当然做不到，因为我和你们所有人一样，都是当今世界的囚徒。

segments.text: We cannot think outside the particular patterns that our brains are conditioned to, or, to be more accurate, we can think only a very little way outside, and then only if we are very

translation.text: 我们无法超越大脑受制约形成的特定模式思考；更准确地说，只能稍微超越一点，而且还必须极富

segments.text: original.

translation.text: 原创性。
